title: [CR] aprs_is.rs: lat_min/lon_min conversion loses precision from fract
description:
File: rust/lichen-gateway/src/aprs_is.rs:289-294

Issue: The lat_min/lon_min calculation uses (lat.abs().fract() * 60.0) as f64 but lat is already f64. The cast is redundant but the real issue is that the result is assigned to a plain f64 variable and then used in format! with :05.2 precision. The calculation itself is correct but the intermediate truncation when extracting the integer degrees via trunc() is lossy.

More importantly, line 290 uses (lat.abs().fract() * 60.0) as f64 but fract() already returns f64 and 60.0 is f64, so the as f64 is a no-op. This is just redundant code.

Idiomatic fix: Remove the redundant as f64 casts.
close_reason: Removed redundant 'as f64' casts in aprs_is.rs lat_min/lon_min calculation
